serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of braised pork intestine with Laba garlic is approximately 150 g, served as a main dish alongside steamed rice.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; a 150 g serving delivers approximately 1575 mg sodium, representing over 68% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit, and the braising liquid (soy sauce, dark soy sauce) and pickled Laba garlic both contribute substantially to this load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.0 g/100g) and zero sugar minimise glycaemic impact, but high sodium and saturated fat require monitoring
type2_diabetes.guidance: The negligible carbohydrate content means this dish poses minimal direct glycaemic risk for patients with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the high sodium content (1050 mg/100g) increases cardiovascular risk in this population, and portion control is advised to limit saturated fat intake from pork intestine.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100g), cholesterol (89 mg/100g), and saturated fat from pork offal elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid this dish due to the combination of very high sodium (1050 mg/100g), dietary cholesterol (89 mg/100g), and saturated fat from pork large intestine, all of which contribute to hypertension, dyslipidaemia, and atherosclerotic progression.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork offal is high in arachidonic acid (pro-inflammatory omega-6); high sodium worsens joint swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid pork intestine as a pork offal product rich in arachidonic acid, which promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; the high sodium content (1050 mg/100g) further exacerbates joint inflammation and fluid retention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised Pork Intestine with Laba Garlic (腊八蒜烧肥肠, Là Bā Suàn Shāo Féi Cháng) is a traditional Beijing dish that pairs slow-braised pork large intestine with Laba garlic — a distinctive Chinese condiment made by pickling garlic cloves in rice vinegar during the Laba Festival (the eighth day of the twelfth lunar month), which turns the garlic emerald green and imparts a sharp, tangy, mildly pungent flavour. The pork intestine is thoroughly cleaned, blanched to remove impurities, then slow-braised in a rich sauce of soy sauce, dark soy sauce, Shaoxing cooking wine, ginger, scallion, sugar, and aromatic spices until tender and deeply flavoured. The finished dish has a soft, slightly chewy texture with a savoury-umami braising glaze and the characteristic sour-pungent note of Laba garlic cutting through the richness of the offal. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, 10.5 g fat, and 0.0 g carbohydrate, with a very high sodium content of 1050 mg — making it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The high protein content reflects the collagen-rich nature of pork intestine. Cholesterol is 89 mg/100g. This dish is served as a main course at lunch or dinner in Beijing-style Chinese restaurants and is particularly associated with the Laba Festival culinary tradition. It is not suitable for halal, kosher, or vegetarian diets.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010138, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cooked pork large intestine as an approved secondary source. All other values preserved from the HPB canonical record.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010138); the braising preparation with small amounts of sugar does not materially alter the per-100g carbohydrate figure at the reported precision. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per the same source. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — pork intestine contains no dietary fibre. These are confirmed zero values, not missing data. Saturated_fat_g (3.6 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 168375 (Pork, large intestine, cooked) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is an approved secondary source substitution. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific braised preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for the exact dish. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value required by the template numeric type contract; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to clarify that GI testing is not meaningful for this food, which contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100g confirmed per HPB). Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by calculation. Laba garlic may contain residual vinegar sugar — vendors should be consulted to confirm the per-100g carbohydrate figure. Confirm no starch thickeners are added to the braising sauce. This dish is not suitable for halal or kosher diets due to pork content.
